name: Upgradeable Vehicle
description: A progression system where players install compatible vehicle parts that change handling, durability, utility, storage, fuel use, or traversal capability.
design_notes: Vehicle upgrades work best when parts create recognizable tradeoffs instead of simple numeric escalation. A heavier armor plate can improve survivability while hurting acceleration; off-road tires can help dirt surfaces without replacing every other tire choice. The implementation should treat installation as an inventory transaction with previewed stat changes, compatibility checks, and persistent vehicle state.
design_purpose: Let players specialize a vehicle for racing, hauling, survival, combat, or exploration.
Connect vehicle handling to economy, crafting, loot, and long-term progression.
Give designers knobs for build diversity without creating a separate vehicle for every playstyle.
player_fantasy: The player feels ownership over a machine they have tuned for their route, role, or driving style.

parameters.name: vehicle_id
parameters.typical_range: stable vehicle archetype or instance id
parameters.description: Vehicle receiving installed parts and saved modifiers.

parameters.name: upgrade_part_id
parameters.description: Part item being installed, removed, or previewed.

parameters.name: part_slot_id
parameters.description: Vehicle slot that constrains compatible parts.

parameters.name: compatibility_rule_id
parameters.typical_range: per vehicle family or slot group
parameters.description: Rule set used before accepting an install transaction.

parameters.name: install_transaction_id
parameters.description: Atomic operation covering item removal, part install, and refund.

parameters.name: stat_modifier_curve_id
parameters.description: Modifier profile applied to vehicle handling or utility stats.

parameters.name: save_migration_id
parameters.description: Compatibility key for old saves after part definitions change.

edge_cases: A content update removes or renames an installed upgrade_part_id in an existing save.
The player previews an upgrade while another menu, teammate, or server request changes the same vehicle.
A part install would exceed storage capacity because removed parts need to return to inventory.
Two upgrades modify the same derived stat through different curves and apply in the wrong order.
A multiplayer vehicle owner disconnects during an install transaction.
common_bugs: The UI preview uses client-only formulas that do not match the authoritative stat modifier pipeline.
Installed parts duplicate when an equip operation succeeds but inventory removal fails.
Vehicle physics caches are not rebuilt after part changes, so new handling appears only after reload.
Compatibility rules check vehicle archetype but not part_slot_id, allowing impossible combinations.
Save migration silently drops unknown parts instead of replacing them with a fallback or refund.
balancing_notes: Purely dominant parts collapse build variety; attach tradeoffs to mass, grip, durability, storage, or fuel use.
Upgrade availability can pace world traversal, but hard-gating too many routes can make vehicles feel like keys instead of tools.
Stat previews should show both gains and penalties so players understand why a heavier build behaves differently.
accessibility_notes: Expose upgrade effects in text as well as bars or icons, especially for color-dependent comparisons.
Let players compare current, previewed, and previous configurations without relying on memory.
Avoid requiring fast menu navigation to swap important parts before timed events.

implementation_notes.unity: Represent parts as ScriptableObjects or addressable data, but perform install_transaction_id handling in a service that checks item_instance_id ownership, part_slot_id compatibility, capacity for removed items, and stat cache rebuilds before saving the vehicle instance.
implementation_notes.godot: Store upgrade definitions as Resources and vehicle instances as saved dictionaries containing slot to upgrade_part_id mappings. Apply stat_modifier_curve_id during a single recompute pass so UI previews, driving state, and persistence use the same derived values.
implementation_notes.unreal: Use Data Assets for part definitions and a replicated VehicleUpgradeComponent for installed slot state. Server RPCs should validate permission, inventory locks, compatibility_rule_id, and rollback behavior before broadcasting changed derived stats to clients.
implementation_notes.web: Keep upgrade definitions in versioned JSON and process installs through an atomic reducer. Persist vehicle_id, part_slot_id, upgrade_part_id, and content version together so browser saves can migrate or refund removed parts deterministically.

example_games.title: Need for Speed Underground
example_games.note: Uses vehicle upgrades as part of racing progression.

example_games.title: Forza Horizon 5
example_games.note: Includes vehicle tuning and part-based performance changes.
